rationale: The agent misses key_collision 6× because all failing scenarios involve a PR that *defines* the cache helper — when no call sites are in the diff, the agent checks the implementation (which looks correct) and moves on, never flagging that the `key: string` API itself enables bare-ID collisions from any future caller; adding an explicit "helper definition" sub-bullet forces the agent to evaluate the API contract, not just call sites.
old_text:
- **Cache key collisions**: keys that are bare IDs with no entity-type prefix — e.g. `cacheGet('42')` used for both users and products hits the same slot; keys must encode the entity type (`user:42`, `product:42`)
    - **Scan every call site, not just the helper**: for each invocation of `cacheGet`/`cacheSet`/`getOrSet` (or any equivalent), read the literal key argument — if you see `cacheGet(userId)`, `cacheGet(String(id))`, `` cacheGet(`${id}`) ``, or any bare numeric/string ID without a type prefix string, flag it as a collision risk even when the cache helper itself looks correct
    - **Multi-tenant check**: if `tenantId` or `orgId` exists in the surrounding scope but the cache key omits it, entries collide across tenants — verify every dimension that makes the resource unique is present in the key
new_text:
- **Cache key collisions**: keys that are bare IDs with no entity-type prefix — e.g. `cacheGet('42')` used for both users and products hits the same slot; keys must encode the entity type (`user:42`, `product:42`)
    - **Scan every call site, not just the helper**: for each invocation of `cacheGet`/`cacheSet`/`getOrSet` (or any equivalent), read the literal key argument — if you see `cacheGet(userId)`, `cacheGet(String(id))`, `` cacheGet(`${id}`) ``, or any bare numeric/string ID without a type prefix string, flag it as a collision risk even when the cache helper itself looks correct
    - **Multi-tenant check**: if `tenantId` or `orgId` exists in the surrounding scope but the cache key omits it, entries collide across tenants — verify every dimension that makes the resource unique is present in the key
    - **Helper API design (when the PR defines the helper, not a call site)**: if the new helper accepts `key: string` with no namespace enforcement — no `key.includes(':')` assertion, no branded `CacheKey` type, no runtime prefix validation — flag the API design itself as a key_collision risk; a caller passing `cacheGet(userId)` or `cacheGet('42')` will silently collide with any other entity type that uses the same numeric ID, and no call site needs to be in the diff to identify this — the absence of a namespace contract in the function signature is the bug
